rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into implementation scope when binding to implementation-level outputs or subcomponent connections.
Place output range guarantees in component type annexes only when using visible features and concrete bounds.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope boundaries: component type annexes use visible features; implementation annexes use internal bindings.
Ensure AGREE expressions reference only explicitly exposed ports or constants, not implicit AADL properties.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be wrapped in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Use `eq` to define helper boolean expressions for range checks before referencing them in guarantees.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Avoid using subcomponent paths or implementation-only identifiers in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement for requirements not tied to visible features
